name: Terms-of-Sale-V2.docx
content:
What are C.E. King’s standard terms of sale and business conditions?

C.E. King supplies machines, spare parts, and services under comprehensive Terms and Conditions (Version 2026.8, effective 13 March 2026). These terms apply to all quotations, orders, and supplies of equipment and services. C.E. King trades exclusively with business customers — consumer purchases are not accepted. Full terms are provided with formal quotations and are available on request from accounts@ceking.com.

Details

  • Business customers only: C.E. King does not sell to consumers; all buyers must be acting in the course of business
  • Payment terms: 30 days from date of invoice unless otherwise stated in the contract; time for payment is of the essence
  • Advance payments (deposits): non-refundable once C.E. King has commenced manufacture, procurement, or allocation of engineering resources
  • Late payment: interest charged at 3% above Bank of England base rate, compounded monthly, plus statutory compensation under the Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Interest) Act 1998
  • Delivery dates: all dates and times for delivery are estimates only and time is not of the essence unless a firm date is expressly agreed; C.E. King is not liable for delays beyond its reasonable control
  • Risk and title: risk in equipment passes to the buyer on delivery; title does not pass until all amounts owed have been paid in full (retention of title)
  • Warranty period: 12 months from delivery or acceptance (whichever is later), or 2,200 working hours, whichever occurs first
  • Warranty scope: equipment will conform to specification, be free from material defects in design, material, and workmanship, and be of satisfactory quality
  • Warranty exclusions: further use after reporting a defect, fair wear and tear, misuse, abnormal storage, failure to follow instructions, or unauthorised alteration or repair
  • Warranty remedy: C.E. King will, at its option, repair or replace defective equipment, or refund the price — this is the buyer’s sole and exclusive remedy
  • Claims process: shortage or visible damage must be reported in writing within 3 business days of delivery; non-delivery within 7 days; hidden defects within 7 business days of discovery and within the warranty period
  • Claims must include: description of defect, photographic/video evidence, equipment serial number(s), and confirmation of access for inspection
  • Acceptance testing: Factory Acceptance Testing (FAT) and Site Acceptance Testing (SAT) carried out where agreed in the contract; equipment deemed accepted 3 business days after delivery if no acceptance testing is agreed
  • Liability: aggregate liability capped at the total contract price paid; no liability for consequential loss, loss of profits, or indirect damages
  • Returns: unused goods may be returned subject to restocking fee — contact support@ceking.com before returning any item
  • International shipping: import duties, taxes, customs clearance, and any documentary/export requirements are the buyer’s responsibility
  • Incoterms: where the contract incorporates Incoterms, the version in force at the date of the contract applies
  • Intellectual property: all IP rights in equipment, software, modifications, and documentation remain with C.E. King; software is licensed, not sold
  • Data protection: C.E. King complies with UK GDPR and Data Protection Act 2018; personal data may be collected via website, Shopify, contact forms, JivoChat, WhatsApp, and other B2B platforms
  • Export control: buyers must comply with all applicable export control laws including UK, EU, and US regulations
  • Anti-bribery and modern slavery: both parties must comply with the Bribery Act 2010 and Modern Slavery Act 2015
  • Dispute resolution: negotiation between senior executives (20 business days) → CEDR mediation (30 business days) → arbitration under the Arbitration Act 1996 with sole arbitrator in London
  • Governing law: laws of England and Wales; exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of England and Wales
  • Force majeure: C.E. King not liable for delays caused by events beyond its reasonable control including acts of God, government action, war, pandemic, cyber-attack, supply chain disruption, or component shortages; either party may terminate if force majeure continues for more than 60 days
  • Variation orders: all changes to equipment, services, or contract scope must be agreed in writing via a formal Variation Order
  • For a full copy of the terms and conditions, contact accounts@ceking.com
  • Spare parts via king.parts: payment is taken at checkout by card
